title: Information Security
description: Hand-written closed questions made file by file from the Information Security Markdown sources.

questions.prompt: What is the main purpose of information security?
choices: Protect information and systems from unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification, or destruction
Remove all controls from an organization
questions.explanation: Information security protects information assets and the systems that process them.

questions.prompt: Which lifecycle is used to develop security controls in an organized way?
questions.explanation: The lecture lists the Security Systems Development Life Cycle as a core topic.

questions.prompt: What is a primary mission of information security?
choices: Ensure systems and contents stay protected and useful
questions.explanation: The lecture connects information security with protecting systems, content, and operations.

questions.prompt: Why are threats studied in information security?
choices: To understand possible dangers and select suitable controls
To remove all users from a system
questions.explanation: Threat understanding is necessary before choosing effective protection.

questions.prompt: An information system consists of which broad components?
choices: Software, hardware, data, people, procedures, and networks
questions.explanation: The lecture says an information system is more than hardware and software.

questions.prompt: What does hardware mean in an information system?
choices: The physical technology used to process, store, and communicate information
questions.explanation: Hardware is the physical part of the information system.

questions.prompt: What does software include in an information system?
choices: Applications, operating systems, and command utilities
questions.explanation: The lecture identifies software as one major component of information systems.

questions.prompt: Why are software failures security-relevant?
choices: Because errors can become threats or vulnerabilities
Because software has no relation to information systems
questions.explanation: Technical software failures or errors are a category of threat.

questions.prompt: What is an attack?
choices: An action that exploits a weakness
questions.explanation: The lecture defines attack as exploiting a weakness.

questions.prompt: What is a vulnerability?
choices: A weakness in a system or control
questions.explanation: A vulnerability is a weakness that can be exploited.

questions.prompt: Who is a hacker in the security context used by the lecture?
choices: A person who accesses systems or information without authorization
A person who prints reports only
questions.explanation: The lecture describes unauthorized system or information access.

questions.prompt: Why are password vulnerabilities important?
choices: Weak passwords can allow unauthorized access
questions.explanation: Password weaknesses are a common path to breaching confidentiality.

questions.prompt: What is antivirus software designed to do?
choices: Detect, block, and remove malicious software
questions.explanation: The lecture names viruses, worms, and trojans as examples of malware.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of intrusion detection and prevention?
choices: Identify suspicious activity and help stop attacks
questions.explanation: Security software helps detect and respond to malicious behavior.

questions.prompt: What is cryptography?
choices: Making and using codes to secure transmission of information
Removing all keys from a system
questions.explanation: Cryptography protects information by transforming it using codes or keys.

questions.prompt: What is cryptanalysis?
choices: Trying to obtain the original message from encrypted data without authorized access
questions.explanation: Cryptanalysis studies breaking or analyzing encrypted messages.

questions.prompt: What is access control concerned with?
choices: Determining whether and how to admit a user into a trusted area or system
questions.explanation: Access control manages admission and permissions.

questions.prompt: Which is an example of a trusted area?
questions.explanation: The lecture lists information systems and restricted computer rooms as trusted areas.

questions.prompt: Why are firewalls used?
choices: To protect networks and sensitive information by controlling traffic
questions.explanation: Firewalls are network protection components.

questions.prompt: What increased the need for network protection?
choices: Rapid growth in digital communication and network threats
questions.explanation: More digital communication increases exposure to attacks.

questions.prompt: What is risk management in information security?
choices: Identifying, assessing, and treating risks to information assets
Ignoring threats until an incident happens
questions.explanation: Risk management connects threats, vulnerabilities, likelihood, impact, and controls.

questions.prompt: Why is risk assessment important?
choices: It helps prioritize controls according to likelihood and impact
It guarantees no attack can happen
questions.explanation: Controls should be chosen based on the importance and level of risk.

exams.title: Reference - Principles of Information Security 4th
exams.description: Manual questions from the 4th edition reference Markdown file.

questions.prompt: Which idea is central in information security references?
choices: Using one password for every system
questions.explanation: The CIA triad is a core framework in information security.

questions.prompt: Why do references discuss policies and controls together?
choices: Policies define expectations and controls help enforce them
Policies are always enough without action
Controls remove the need for management
questions.explanation: Security needs both direction and implementation.

exams.title: Reference - Principles of Information Security 7th
exams.description: Manual questions from the 7th edition reference Markdown file.

questions.prompt: What should an organization protect as information assets?
choices: Data, systems, people, processes, and supporting technology
questions.explanation: Modern security treats information assets broadly, not as software alone.

questions.prompt: Why do security controls need continuous review?
choices: Threats, vulnerabilities, and business needs change over time
Policies become permanent after one draft
questions.explanation: Security is an ongoing management process.

exams.title: Reference - أمن المعلومات بلغة ميسرة
exams.description: Manual questions from the Arabic reference Markdown file.

questions.prompt: ما الفكرة العامة لأمن المعلومات؟
choices: حماية المعلومات من الوصول أو التعديل أو الإتلاف غير المصرح به
questions.explanation: أمن المعلومات يركز على حماية السرية والسلامة والتوافر.

questions.prompt: لماذا يجب تبسيط مفاهيم أمن المعلومات للمستخدمين؟
choices: لأن سلوك المستخدم جزء مهم من الحماية
لأن المستخدمين لا يؤثرون في الأمن
questions.explanation: الفهم الجيد يساعد المستخدم على تجنب الأخطاء والهجمات الاجتماعية.
